data.title: Western Union Acquires Singapore-Based Digital Wallet Company Dash
data.description: Western Union has completed the acquisition of Dash, a digital wallet company based in Singapore. According to BlockBeats, the financial details of the transaction have not been disclosed. Dash was previously owned by Singtel. This acquisition marks Western Union's first digital wallet presence in the Asia-Pacific region and signifies the company's expansion beyond its traditional remittance services into digital financial services.

data.title: Western Union completes acquisition of Singapore digital wallet Dash
data.description: Western Union announced the completion of its acquisition of Dash, the digital wallet owned by Singtel. This transaction gives Western Union its first digital wallet in the Asia-Pacific region and is an important part of its Beyond strategy. Founded in 2014, Dash has over 1.4 million users in Singapore, offering services such as bill payment, overseas remittances, savings, investment, and insurance. Western Union stated that integrating Dash will allow its Singaporean customers to connect more directly to its global network covering more than 200 countries and regions. Vince Tallent, Head of Western Union Asia Pacific, said that combining Dash's local customer base with Western Union's global network will help provide a more seamless service. The specific amount of the transaction was not disclosed.

data.title: Bitcoin Network Advocates for Conservative Node Software to Enhance Decentralization
data.description:
The Bitcoin network is urged to adopt a conservative approach to its client node software implementation to maintain its monetary properties and bolster network decentralization. According to Cointelegraph, Jimmy Song, co-founder of ProductionReady, a non-profit organization dedicated to funding open-source Bitcoin node software development and education, emphasizes the importance of cautious code changes. Song notes that the organization is biased against significant code modifications unless there is overwhelming community support.

Song articulates a guiding principle: if a change does not clearly improve the monetary aspect of Bitcoin, it should not be implemented. ProductionReady aims to restore the 83-byte OP_Return data limit for non-monetary information in Bitcoin transactions, highlighting the necessity of keeping node storage costs low to ensure decentralization. Song explains that the more self-sovereign Bitcoin users become, the more decentralized and resilient the network is. This requires maintaining low costs for running a node, allowing ordinary individuals to participate.

The Bitcoin network's decentralization is strengthened by maximizing nodes and making them accessible to average users, thereby reducing the risk of false transactions or network centralization through collusion. Bitcoin Core remains the preferred software for node operators, with 77.8% of the network running some version of the Core software, while 21.8% run Bitcoin Knots.

In 2025, node storage and on-chain spam became contentious issues after Bitcoin Core developers altered the 83-byte data limit in Bitcoin Core version 30, the latest major upgrade to the reference implementation for Bitcoin node software. Despite significant community opposition, the limit was increased to 100,000 bytes. The proposal to change the limit received four times as many downvotes as upvotes on its GitHub pull request page.

The release of Bitcoin Core 30 in October 2025 led to a historic surge in the number of Bitcoin nodes running Bitcoin Knots, an alternative node client software. The number of Bitcoin Knots nodes reached record highs, representing over 21.7% of nodes on the network, according to Coin Dance. Prior to the decision to remove the OP_Return function, only about 1% of the network was running the Knots software in 2024.

data.title: Project 0 Resumes Operations After Drift Protocol Hack
data.description:
Decentralized lending protocol Project 0 has announced the resumption of its operations. According to Foresight News, the platform had previously halted activities and initiated a deleveraging process following a hack on the Drift protocol. Project 0 reported that the remaining socialized loss amounts to approximately $1.9147 million, which will be distributed across the credit pool, resulting in an average asset write-down of 2.61% for lenders.

The write-down percentages vary by asset risk level, with USDC, SOL, and USDT experiencing a 1% reduction, mainstream assets like BTC and ETH seeing a 2.61% decrease, and WIF, BONK, and governance tokens facing a 6.62% reduction. Project 0 noted that as Drift assets are gradually unlocked and returned to affected users, the actual loss will be less than the stated baseline figures.

Previously, Project 0 co-founder MacBrennan explained that the protocol employs a unified credit pool to enable cross-protocol margin functionality, which is a core mechanism of P0. However, some community members expressed dissatisfaction, stating that they were impacted despite not directly participating in Drift lending.

data.title: Polymarket Apologizes for Controversial Prediction Market on Downed U.S. Fighter Jet
data.description:
Polymarket has issued an apology for allowing users to speculate on the fate of a U.S. fighter jet pilot after the aircraft was shot down over Iran. According to Foresight News, NBC reported that a U.S. official confirmed a two-seater F-15E Strike Eagle was downed on Friday, with one crew member rescued and the other still missing.

The now-deleted market allowed users to predict when the pilot would be rescued, with most speculating a Saturday rescue. The market was titled, "U.S. Confirms Pilot Will Be Rescued By...?" U.S. Representative Seth Moulton, a former Marine Corps veteran who served in Iraq, criticized the market on social media platform X, highlighting the inappropriateness of such speculation during a dangerous search and rescue operation in Iran.

In response to Moulton's post on X, Polymarket apologized and stated that the market had been removed. The company explained, "We have immediately taken down the market as it does not meet our integrity standards. This content should not have been published, and we are investigating how it bypassed our internal review mechanisms."

data.title: Kuwait: Two power and desalination plants attacked by Iranian drones
data.description: Kuwait officials stated that two power and desalination plants were attacked by Iranian drones, causing "significant" material damage, and two generator units have been shut down. (Jinshi)

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. A new address establishes a position of 2,015 ETH, worth $4.13 million; 2. Economists: The Federal Reserve may find it difficult to cut interest rates this year; 3. The Ethereum Foundation has staked 46,000 ETH; 4. Iran: If the situation escalates, the entire Middle East will be hell for the US and Israel; 5. The Iranian military rejects Trump's "48-hour" ultimatum; 6. Bitcoin at Block, founded by Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey, will launch a free Bitcoin faucet; 7. Michael Saylor: A global consensus has formed that "Bitcoin is digital capital"; 8. A suspected Bitmine address adds another 40,000 ETH, worth approximately $82.07 million; 9. Trump issues another 48-hour ultimatum to Iran.

data.title: Kuwait Reports Significant Damage from Iranian Drone Attack on Power and Desalination Plants
data.description: Kuwait has reported that two of its power and desalination plants were attacked by Iranian drones, resulting in significant material damage. According to Odaily, the assault led to the shutdown of two generator units. The incident underscores ongoing regional tensions and the vulnerability of critical infrastructure to drone warfare.

data.title: Block Co-Founder Jack Dorsey Introduces Decentralized P2P Inference Project
data.description:
Block co-founder Jack Dorsey has introduced mesh-llm, a decentralized peer-to-peer inference project developed by Block employee Michael Neale. According to Foresight News, this initiative allows users to pool idle computing power to run large open-source models.

Mesh-llm supports popular open-source models such as GLM, Qwen3, DeepSeek, and Llama. It operates under the MIT open-source license and provides an OpenAI-compatible API. The project can integrate with AI programming tools like goose and Claude Code and is currently in the experimental phase.

data.title: Iran Vows to Defend National Rights Against U.S. Threats
data.description:
Iran's military leadership has responded to U.S. President Donald Trump's recent ultimatum, emphasizing their commitment to defending national rights and protecting state assets. According to BlockBeats, Abdollahi, commander of Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, stated on April 4 that Iran will ensure aggressors face consequences.

Iran's Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting reported Abdollahi's remarks, describing Trump's actions as desperate and reckless attempts to threaten Iran's infrastructure and national assets following a series of failures.

Abdollahi warned that if the U.S. and Israel initiate attacks, Iran will launch unrestricted and devastating strikes on all U.S. military infrastructure and Israeli facilities. He reminded both nations that Iran has consistently acted on its declarations since the onset of the conflict imposed on Iran.

data.title: Anthropic Seeks to Establish Employee-Funded Political Action Committee
data.description:
Anthropic has filed documents with the Federal Election Commission to establish an employee-funded political action committee, AnthroPAC. According to Odaily, this move comes amid a dispute with U.S. President Donald Trump's administration over the use of Anthropic's AI system, Claude, for military purposes. Previously, Anthropic refused a Pentagon request to remove safeguards preventing the system's use in mass surveillance or autonomous lethal weapons.

In March, Anthropic sued the federal government after being designated as a national security supply chain risk, a decision that has been temporarily halted by a recent court ruling. Other tech giants like Google, Microsoft, and Amazon have already established similar PACs.

In 2026, Anthropic donated $20 million to Public First Action, an organization supporting AI safety measures.

data.title: Iran Rejects Trump's Ultimatum, Threatens Retaliation
data.description: Iranian military commander Abdollahi has dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump's 48-hour ultimatum, asserting that Iran will protect its national rights and state assets. According to NS3.AI, Abdollahi warned that Iran is prepared to carry out sustained strikes on U.S. and Israeli infrastructure if either country initiates attacks. He emphasized that Iran has already acted on previous wartime warnings since the onset of the conflict.

data.title: New Ethereum Address Acquires 2,015 ETH Worth $4.13 Million
data.description: A new Ethereum address has recently acquired 2,015 ETH, valued at $4.13 million. According to ChainCatcher, the purchase was made 11 hours ago at a price of $2,049.53 per ETH. The acquired Ethereum has since been transferred to another address for storage.

data.title: Economists: Federal Reserve may find it difficult to cut interest rates this year
data.description: Economists say the sudden surge in gasoline prices, felt by American consumers, will be fully reflected in key inflation data released this week. The US March CPI is expected to rise 1% month-over-month, the largest monthly increase since 2022; core CPI is likely to rise 0.3% month-over-month. The Iran war previously pushed up gasoline prices at US gas stations by about $1 per gallon. The day before the CPI data release, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge will provide information on pre-war price pressures. Economists expect the core PCE price index to rise 0.4% for the third consecutive month in February, suggesting that even before the conflict, the process of inflation falling to a more moderate level had stalled. Combined with signs of stabilization in the US labor market, persistent price pressures, and new inflationary risks from the Middle East war, this helps explain why the Federal Reserve may find it difficult to lower interest rates this year. (Jinshi)

data.title: A new address has opened a position of 2015 ETH, worth $4.13 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a new address opened a position of 2015 ETH 11 hours ago, worth $4.13 million, with a withdrawal price of $2049.53. The ETH has now been transferred to another address.

data.title: Economists Predict Significant Rise in U.S. March CPI Amid Gasoline Price Surge
data.description:
Economists have indicated that the sudden increase in gasoline prices felt by U.S. consumers will be prominently reflected in the key inflation data to be released this week. According to Jin10, the U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) for March is expected to rise by 1% month-on-month, marking the largest single-month increase since 2022. The core CPI is anticipated to increase by 0.3% month-on-month. Previously, the Iran conflict led to a rise of approximately $1 per gallon in gasoline prices at U.S. gas stations.

On the day before the CPI data release, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure will provide insights into pre-conflict price pressures. Economists expect the core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index to have risen by 0.4% in February for the third consecutive month, indicating that the process of inflation easing to more moderate levels had already stalled before the conflict began.

The combination of signs of stabilization in the U.S. labor market, persistent price pressures, and new inflation risks from the Middle East conflict helps explain why the Federal Reserve may find it challenging to lower interest rates this year.

data.title: An account with a winning percentage of only 42% purchased $490,000. Arizona defeated Michigan in the NCAA Final Four.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that in the Polymarket "NCAA Final Four Arizona vs. Michigan" prediction event, an account with a winning percentage of only 42.1% (address: 0xfa41acf573021e64b607b3294bd0c0518884a7a1) purchased $490,000 worth of bets on Arizona defeating Michigan, with an average opening price of 50¢. The University of Michigan vs. the University of Arizona game will tip off at 8:49 AM Beijing time this morning. The biggest suspense of this game lies in whether Arizona can use its interior advantage to suppress Michigan's efficient offense, and whether Michigan's three-point shooting can break through Arizona's top-notch defense. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before prices are set.

data.title: Iran Warns of Escalation Impact on Middle East
data.description: Iran has issued a warning that any escalation in tensions could turn the entire Middle East into a hostile environment for the United States and Israel. According to Odaily, this statement comes amid rising geopolitical tensions in the region. Iran's response highlights the potential for widespread instability if the situation deteriorates further.

data.title: Former UK Chancellor Warns of Economic Challenges
data.description:
Kwasi Kwarteng, who briefly served as the UK's Chancellor of the Exchequer in September 2022, has expressed concerns about the country's current fiscal situation. According to PANews, Kwarteng warned that the UK is trapped in a fiscal 'vicious cycle,' where government spending exceeds tax revenue, and rising taxes could ultimately stifle economic growth. He also criticized the prevalent short-termism in politics and markets.

Kwarteng highlighted that during his tenure, both the Treasury and the Bank of England were aware of Bitcoin and digital assets, yet their focus on these innovations remains minimal. He suggested that the UK is reluctant to embrace innovation.

data.title: Arizona vs. Michigan NCAA Final Four Matchup Sparks Betting Interest
data.description:
A significant wager has been placed on the NCAA Final Four matchup between Arizona and Michigan, with a particular account investing $490,000 on Arizona's victory. According to Odaily, the account, which has a win rate of only 42.1%, opened its position at an average price of 50¢.

The game is set to begin at 8:49 AM UTC+8, with the main intrigue revolving around whether Arizona can leverage its inside advantage to counter Michigan's efficient offense. Additionally, Michigan's three-point shooting will be tested against Arizona's top-tier defense.
